novice_linear.description: Add load every session while all target reps are completed.
novice_linear.stall_definition: 2 consecutive failed sessions on the lift
novice_linear.on_stall: micro-deload the lift 10%, rebuild; after 3rd stall cycle, graduate slot to double_progression

double_progression.description: Work within the rep range at fixed load; when ALL sets hit the top of the range, add load and reset to the bottom.
double_progression.stall_definition: no rep or load increase across 3 consecutive sessions of the lift
double_progression.on_stall: swap to next exercise in the pattern ladder for 4 weeks OR micro-deload 7.5% (engine alternates; coach may override)

top_set_backoff.description: One top set progresses by rep target; when top-rep target is hit, load increases. Back-off sets are a fixed % of the top set.
top_set_backoff.stall_definition: top-set e1RM flat or down across 3 sessions
top_set_backoff.on_stall: deload week for the lift (-15% load, -1 set), then resume with backoff_pct 0.80 for one week

rep_progression.description: Bodyweight: add 1-2 reps per set each session up to the rep cap, then advance to the next-harder variation in the pattern ladder and reset reps.
rep_progression.on_stall: hold variation, add a set (max +1), or slow tempo 3-1-1

timed_progression.description: Add 10-15s per set up to 75s, then advance variation.

onramp_pct.description: Return-to-training ramp: weeks 1-4 at 60/70/80/90% of last-known working loads (or RPE 6 cap if unknown). No PR attempts. Graduates to the matched standard archetype at week 5.

readiness_modulation.yellow: remove 1 back-off/accessory set per compound slot; loads unchanged
readiness_modulation.red: technique session: 80% of planned loads, volume -30%, no top sets; suggest swap to rest day if 2nd consecutive red
readiness_modulation.note: readiness NEVER auto-increases load or volume; upside adjustments are coach suggestions requiring user acceptance

deload.triggered: program-level deload when 2+ lifts stall in the same week OR readiness red 3+ days in a 7-day window
deload.scheduled: mandatory deload in week 7 of any block if none was triggered earlier
deload.prescription: volume -40% (sets), intensity -10% (load), no top sets, duration 1 week

safety_bounds.novice_level_cap: slots for experience='new' may only select exercises with level <= 1
safety_bounds.pain_flag: any pain report on a lift freezes its progression and surfaces see-a-professional guidance; engine offers pattern-ladder swap
safety_bounds.hard_rule: the LLM coach proposes; only this rules engine writes loads/sets. All LLM-proposed changes are validated against these bounds.

graduation.novice_to_intermediate: graduate when 3 slots have hit their 3rd linear stall cycle, or at week 12, whichever first
graduation.blocks: all archetypes run 12-week blocks; week 12 is a fitness-test/deload hybrid, then re-plan

rules.note: always on-ramp first; graduates to the otherwise-matched archetype at week 5

rules.fallback_policy: if requested days unavailable for a combination, round DOWN to the nearest archetype (adherence beats volume); never round a 'new' user up past 4 days

archetypes.name: Foundations 3×
archetypes.summary: Alternating A/B full-body. Linear progression on the big lifts — the fastest strength returns a novice will ever see.

slots.note: single heavy set; deadlift recovers slowly

archetypes.notes: Sessions alternate A,B,A / B,A,B week to week.

archetypes.name: Full-Body 3× Growth
archetypes.summary: Three distinct full-body days; every muscle hit 3×/week at moderate volume per session.

archetypes.name: The 2-Day
archetypes.summary: Two efficient full-body sessions. Designed for adherence — covers every pattern, ~45 minutes each.

archetypes.name: Upper / Lower 4× — Strength
archetypes.summary: Two strength days (top-set/back-off), two volume days. The flagship intermediate program — the one in the app's demo.

archetypes.name: Upper / Lower 4× — Growth
archetypes.summary: Hypertrophy-biased upper/lower: higher rep ranges, more isolation, every muscle 2×/week at 12-18 weekly sets.

archetypes.name: Push / Pull / Legs 6×
archetypes.summary: The classic six-day PPL, each muscle 2×/week. High commitment; only offered at 5+ days and 3+ years experience.

archetypes.name: Hybrid 5× (PPL + Upper/Lower)
archetypes.summary: Push/Pull/Legs early week, Upper/Lower to finish. The best-balanced 5-day option.

archetypes.name: Big-Three 3×
archetypes.summary: Squat / bench / deadlift specialization, top-set + back-off everywhere. For lifters who want numbers, not pump.

archetypes.name: Deficit 3× + Conditioning
archetypes.summary: Full-body strength to defend muscle in a calorie deficit, plus short conditioning finishers. Progression expectation: maintain, don't PR.
archetypes.notes: Engine caps load increases at 1 step per 2 weeks during an active deficit; stall rules relaxed (maintenance = success).

archetypes.name: Restart Ramp 3×
archetypes.summary: Four-week on-ramp after a layoff: same A/B structure as Foundations, loads start at 60% of your last-known numbers and climb 10%/week. Soreness-managed, ego-managed. Graduates automatically into the matched standard archetype.

archetypes.name: Garage Full-Body 3×
archetypes.summary: Barbell + rack + bench, no machines. Linear early, double progression after.

archetypes.name: Garage Upper / Lower 4×
archetypes.summary: The flagship UL4, machine-free: every machine slot resolved to its barbell/bodyweight swap.

archetypes.name: Dumbbell Full-Body 3×
archetypes.summary: Dumbbells + bench. Double progression throughout — DB jumps are big, so reps do the early work.

archetypes.name: Dumbbell Upper / Lower 4×
archetypes.summary: Four-day dumbbell split for home lifters who outgrew full-body.

archetypes.name: Dumbbell Deficit 3×
archetypes.summary: The deficit program for the home gym: strength defense + swings.
archetypes.notes: Same deficit progression caps as cut3_fullbody.

archetypes.name: Bodyweight Foundations 3×
archetypes.summary: No equipment. Rep progression into harder variations — the variation ladder IS the barbell.

archetypes.name: Bodyweight 4× Split
archetypes.summary: Upper/lower bodyweight split with advanced progressions (archer push-ups, pistols, nordics).

archetypes.name: Balanced 3× (Strength + Conditioning)
archetypes.summary: For 'I just want to be fit': two strength-led days, one mixed day, conditioning woven in. Optimized for feeling good, not maxing.
